channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israel on Friday launched an attack on the Lebanese capital, Beirut, for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war in November.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Latest: Major earthquakes strike Myanmar and Thailand
item.description: BANGKOK, Thailand (AP) — A 7.7 magnitude earthquake and an aftershock measuring 6.4 rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar on Friday, bringing down a high-rise building under construction in Bangkok and damaging historic buildings in Myanmar. Damage was also reported in China.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about the U.S. military's Pituffik Space Base in Greenland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — The remote Pituffik Space Base in northwestern Greenland, which Vice President JD Vance is visiting on Friday, is the U.S. Department of Defense's northernmost installation. The base was built following a 1951 defense agreement between Denmark and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korea's worst wildfires are now almost contained following rain and cooler weather
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The most destructive wildfires ever to hit South Korea were almost contained, authorities announced Friday, after rain and cooler temperatures helped fire crew put out the blazes that have killed 28 people and razed vast swaths of land since last week.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under U.N.-sponsored external governance, boasts battlefield gains
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has proposed putting Ukraine under external governance under the U.N. aegis as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China's Xi makes strong push for greater foreign investment as economy continues to lag
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping has urged foreign investors to have faith in China's business prospects in an address to major global business leaders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Myanmar and Thailand, killing at least 3 in Bangkok high-rise collapse
item.description: A powerful earthquake has rocked Myanmar and neighboring Thailand, killing at least three people in Bangkok and burying dozens when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A remote Australian town seeks a doctor, offering a $400,000 salary and free rent
item.description: A remote Australian town that will soon lose its only doctor is offering a salary package of up to $680,000 Australian dollars, plus free rent and a car, to attract a new candidate.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the Houthi rebels early Friday, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Lithuanian, Polish troops aid in 'search and recovery' for 4 missing US soldiers from Fort Stewart
item.description: A Lithuanian official told Fox News Digital about ongoing search efforts to recover four missing U.S. Army soldiers in coordination with Poland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia to hold general elections on May 3 with inflation and a housing shortage major issues
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australians will go to the polls on May 3 for general elections with high costs of living and a shortage of housing likely weighing against the government as Prime Minister Anthony Albanese 's center-left Labor Party seeks a second three-year term.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US-Canada airline bookings plummet 70 percent: Report
item.description: Airline bookings between the U.S. and Canada are down by 70 percent compared to the same time last year, according to the aviation analytics company OAG.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Putin predicts Trump will 'systematically' go after Greenland
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Thursday the Trump administration likely will move to acquire Greenland “systemically” after months of swipes at the autonomous territory within the Kingdom of Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: French president says not all European allies agree on a proposed force for Ukraine
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France and Britain will continue to forge ahead with plans to deploy troops in Ukraine to defend an eventual peace deal with Russia but only some other nations want to take part, French President Emmanuel Macron said Thursday after a summit of countries that have been mulling the propos
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, SAMUEL PETREQUIN and BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 6 dead after tourist submarine sinks off Egypt's coast: officials
item.description: Six dead, dozens rescued after tourist submarine sinks off the coast on Egypt on Thursday. Officials say all of the victims were Russian. It is not known what caused the sinking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: His brothers’ keeper: Liran Berman calls for urgent action to save his brothers, Gali and Ziv, from Hamas
item.description: Gali and Ziv Berman were taken hostage on Oct. 7, 2023, and their brother, Liran, has been fighting for their freedom ever since. He says his brothers were "the light of every room" they walked into.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France's foreign minister is in China to discuss Ukraine and trade issues
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot began a two-day visit to China on Thursday and held talks with his Chinese counterpart on Ukraine and longstanding trade disputes between China and Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil ex-President Bolsonaro will stand trial over an alleged coup plan. Here’s what happens next
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices unanimously ruled that former President Jair Bolsonaro and seven of his associates will stand trial on five counts, including attempting to stage a coup after the far-right leader lost the 2022 election.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Sudanese army surrounds Khartoum airport in battle for capital, military sources say
item.description: The Sudanese army is encircling Khartoum airport as it battles to oust its rival Rapid Support Forces from its final foothold in Sudan's capital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: WGN 9

item.title: Two military jets collide in midair stunt, crash to ground in fiery explosion
item.description: Two Alpha jets collided midair during a training exercise in France before crashing to the ground and exploding in a massive fireball, dramatic video shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Hamas protests break out in Gaza Strip over demands to end war
item.description: Palestinians protested in northern Gaza, where they reportedly demanded an end to the war in anti-Hamas protests. The last major protest against the terror group occurred in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US holds separate talks with Russians after meeting Ukrainians to discuss a potential ceasefire
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. negotiators worked on a proposed partial ceasefire in the 3-year-old war in Ukraine on Monday, meeting representatives from Russia one day after holding separate talks with a team from Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US, Russian officials meet in Saudi Arabia to talk ceasefire
item.description: Officials hope the talks will allow negotiators to iron out details of a possible limited ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis is back home after a 5-week hospital stay for life-threatening double pneumonia
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A frail Pope Francis returned to the Vatican on Sunday after a five-week hospitalization for life-threatening double pneumonia, and he made a surprise stop at his favorite basilica on the way home before beginning two months of prescribed rest and recovery.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, PAOLO SANTALUCIA and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Oleg Gordievsky, Britain's most valuable Cold War spy inside the KGB, dies at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Oleg Gordievsky, a Soviet KGB officer who helped change the course of the Cold War by covertly passing secrets to Britain, has died. He was 86. Gordievsky died March 4 in England, where he had lived since defecting in 1985.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday, doctors say
item.description: Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday, after 38 days battling a life-threatening case of pneumonia in both lungs, his doctors said.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 convicted in US of Iran-backed plot to kill journalist critical of Tehran
item.description: Prosecutors said Iran’s elite Revolutionary Guard Corps paid Amirov and Omarov $500,000 for the botched hit.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: London's Heathrow slowly resumes flights after a fire cut power to Europe's busiest airport
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A fire at an electrical substation knocked out power to Heathrow Airport for most of Friday, forcing Europe's busiest hub to shut down for roughly 18 hours, causing widespread cancellations and rerouting headaches, and stranding roughly 200,000 passengers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, DANICA KIRKA and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Russian drones strike Ukrainian city of Odesa, underlining challenges for even limited truce
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian drones pummeled the Ukrainian Black Sea port city of Odesa, injuring three people and sparking massive fires, officials said Friday, an attack that underlined Moscow's intention to pursue aerial strikes even as it agreed to temporarily halt strikes on energy facilities.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9
